title: AWS Credential Providers, Abort Signals, Retries, and Errors
description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A process accepts static keys from request data, retries every failure beyond its caller deadline, and records neither attempt identity nor ambiguous write state. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or promise-level claim. Learners cumulatively produce typed request and retry state machine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use short-lived credential providers with explicit workload identity, refresh ownership, least privilege, and redaction.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, explain SigV4 canonical request, region, service, signed headers, payload mode, clock, and credential scope without hand-building production signatures.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, compose request, attempt, transfer, and shutdown deadlines and propagate cancellation through body and async task ownership.
